subject: Science
chapter: Adolescence: Stage of Growth and Change

key_points: Adolescence is the period of life when the body undergoes changes leading to reproductive maturity.
It usually begins around the age of 11 and lasts until 18 or 19 years of age.
Puberty is the process of physical changes by which a child's body matures into an adult body.
Growth in height is one of the most noticeable changes during puberty.
The changes occurring during adolescence are controlled by chemical substances called hormones.
examples: Physical changes: Sudden increase in height, change in body shape, and voice change.
Voice change: The voice box (larynx) grows larger, sometimes seen as Adam's apple in boys.
Skin changes: Increased activity of sweat and sebaceous (oil) glands often leads to acne or pimples.
Emotional changes: Adolescents may experience mood swings and become more self-conscious.
formulas: Calculation of full height: (Present height / % of full height at this age) × 100
Endocrine Action: Endocrine Gland → Hormone → Target Site
exam_tips: Remember that the Adam's apple is specifically the protruding part of the throat in boys.
Note that the rate of growth in height varies from person to person.
Understand that adolescence is a natural process and not a cause for worry.
Identify that sebaceous glands are responsible for oily skin during this phase.
